examples: One believer is Life Technologies, a big sequencer manufacturer.
In 1986, Leroy Hood published a prototype design for world's first automated DNA sequencer.
In June 1986, Leroy Hood, then at Caltech, published his prototype design for an automated DNA sequencer.
This works on the same principle as the old sequencer, but does things more efficiently.
Your choice of a sequencer may very well depend on the plug-ins you want to use.
